judgment.summary: Hims & Hers shows strong gross margins and positive free cash flow, but faces declining earnings estimates and significant debt.
judgment.earningsRecap: Last reported quarter EPS was -0.40, missing the estimate of 0.03 by -1267.9%. Next earnings date is 2026-08-10.
positives: Gross margin of 72.9% indicates strong pricing power or efficient cost of goods sold.
Free cash flow of $174.31M (TTM) shows the company is generating cash from operations after capital expenditures.
Total cash of $750.88M provides a cushion for short-term obligations.
Analyst mean target of $26.46 suggests potential upside from the current spot price of $22.44.
negatives: Operating margin of -7.9% and profit margin of -0.6% indicate the company is not currently profitable on an operating basis.
Debt/Equity ratio of 253.72 is very high, suggesting significant financial leverage.
Analyst growth estimates for the current quarter (-54.5%) and current fiscal year (-15.3%) are negative.
Return on equity of -2.7% shows the company is not effectively generating profits from shareholder investments.
Beta of 2.42 indicates higher volatility compared to the broader market.

news.paragraph: Hims & Hers Health recently announced plans for an upsized $350 million convertible senior notes offering, intended to fund international expansion and AI investments. This news has led to a significant drop in the stock price, with headlines also noting a substantial earnings miss and the CEO selling a large number of shares prior to this event. Traders should consider the implications of this dilutive financing and the negative price reaction when evaluating options contracts.
